originalBehavior: The published article loads through a shared article shell plus the page-local base and sound scripts, then initializes many inline keyboard, waveform, particle, and pressure-wave sections under one longform page.
localStatus: The local route keeps the published article structure, shared article styling, and both vendored scripts while rewriting static-hosting metadata and asset paths for the subpath-hosted replica.
notes: The public body no longer exposes the upstream author banner, blog navigation, analytics, or footer supporter prompt.
The route keeps the original longform article structure instead of condensing the content into a custom local rewrite.
evidence: Implementation planning treated the published article shell and both scripts as parity-critical.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the route loads locally, both scripts initialize, and the core interactive sections mount without runtime errors.

originalBehavior: The published article introduces keyboard-driven sound scenes in which clicking keys or pressing W, E, and R triggers plate motion, waveform changes, and synthesized audio output.
localStatus: The local route keeps the published keyboard widgets, key-state handling, waveform plots, and synthesized audio behavior for the early sound and waveform scenes.
notes: The keyboard scenes still rely on the original KeyBoard and Drawer runtime rather than a local rewrite.
The route remains usable when autoplay is blocked because the published runtime warms the audio graph only after user interaction.
evidence: Implementation planning treated click-driven keyboard input and W/E/R keyboard input as smoke-critical.
Shared smoke coverage verifies a waveform keyboard click redraws the scene and that W routes to the published top-of-viewport keyboard with the expected pressed-state behavior.

originalBehavior: The published article continues into particle simulations, pressure-box resizing scenes, slider-driven waveform controls, and later additive-wave visualizations with play or pause controls.
localStatus: The local route keeps the particle drag scenes, pressure and length sliders, waveform-addition visualizations, and published animation controls without replacing them with custom local components.
notes: The particle and pressure scenes still rely on the original canvas and GL drawer runtime.
Published play or pause controls remain in place for the animated scenes that expose them.
evidence: Implementation planning treated one early drag scene, one pressure or box-length slider scene, one additive waveform scene, and one play or pause control as parity-critical.

Shared smoke coverage verifies a particle canvas redraws on drag, a pressure-box slider redraws the active scene, a later waveform-addition slider updates the plot, and a published play or pause button toggles state.

originalBehavior: The published page relies on article-local helper sprites and shared control icons while also exposing author promo links, RSS, and supporter surfaces around the article shell.
localStatus: The local copy vendors the helper sprites locally, keeps the keyboard and control surfaces intact, and leaves the shared footer as the only public original-page reference surface.
notes: The local copy uses local fallback font stacks so the route does not depend on runtime Google Fonts requests.
The route synthesizes sound in-browser and does not require separate upstream audio file requests at runtime.
evidence: The public-surface audit blocks author-promo, analytics, and supporter surfaces from shipping in the route body.
Shared smoke coverage and route checks verify the route loads locally without upstream asset requests while keeping the interactive controls usable.
